response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial experience (founder of GISCollective, Green Map GIS work) and low-latency systems background (DLang microservices handling millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby), which map well to routing data pipelines. However, modern C++ exposure is dated (National Instruments, 2014-2015) and recent years have centered on TypeScript/Node full-stack product work rather than systems programming. No direct navigation/routing tile experience. DLang proficiency suggests he could ramp into C++ or Rust, but he is not an immediate fit for a C++-primary role.
strengths: Deep geospatial domain experience via GISCollective and Green Map GIS platforms
Proven low-latency, high-throughput systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching in DLang)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling authorship
concerns: Modern C++ experience is roughly a decade old; recent work is TypeScript/Node
No direct routing, navigation guidance, or map tiling pipeline experience
Recent roles skew toward full-stack product work rather than distributed data pipelines
